summary: Matched several Kirby copy-ability SpecialN routines in ftKb_SpecialNYs.c, adding implementations for ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC, ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868, and ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0, and finishing ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim. The decomp.dev report credited 4 new matches and +460 bytes, raising matched code by +0.01%. Evidence is limited to the diff and bot match report; there were no human review comments.
what_changed: Replaced the nonmatching marker for ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC with code that checks fp->target_item_gobj, decides a bool has_destroy_type from specific Yoshi-copy capture motion IDs, calls it_802F2E7C on the target item, then clears fp->x1A64 and fp->target_item_gobj.
Added inline helper getAirSpecialMotionId that selects ftKb_MS_MsSpecialAirNStart when Kirby's hat kind is FTKIND_MARS and ftKb_MS_FeSpecialAirNStart otherwise.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868 as an air transition: calls ftCommon_8007D5D4(fp), then Fighter_ChangeMotionState with the selected air SpecialN start motion, flags 0x0C4C7084, current animation frame, 1.0f rate, 0.0f blend, and NULL.
Added inline helper getGroundSpecialMotionId with the same Marth-versus-Fire Emblem selection pattern for grounded motion IDs ftKb_MS_MsSpecialNStart and ftKb_MS_FeSpecialNStart.

Implemented ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0 as a grounded transition: calls ftCommon_8007D7FC(fp), then Fighter_ChangeMotionState with the selected ground SpecialN start motion and the same flags/frame/rate/blend arguments.
Adjusted ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim from a u32* charge to s32* charge with explicit casts from the dat_attrs charge_iterations fields.

Changed ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim so that after cur_frame exceeds charge_iterations * 30 it sets fp->cmd_vars[0] = 1 and calls ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148(gobj), replacing the prior ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining(gobj) call.
smart_moves: Factored Marth-versus-Fire Emblem motion-ID selection into tiny inline helpers, preserving a simple call shape for Fighter_ChangeMotionState while avoiding duplicated conditional expressions in the matched routines.

Kept the item cleanup routine narrowly ordered: only when target_item_gobj exists, compute the destroy-type bool from exact capture motion IDs, notify the item with it_802F2E7C, then clear both fighter item references.
Used explicit s32* casts for charge_iterations selection in ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim, suggesting the signedness and pointer type were important to the remaining match.

Replaced a generic animation-frame query call with ftKb_SpecialNPe_8010C148 in the loop-completion path, which was enough to take ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim from 97.69% to 100% according to the bot report.
decomp_lessons: For Kirby copied SpecialN code, motion selection can hinge on fp->fv.kb.hat.kind, here specifically FTKIND_MARS versus the fallback Fire Emblem path.
When matching small transition functions, isolate motion-ID selection if it helps the compiler produce the expected call sequence around Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Preserve exact motion-state arguments, including magic flag constants such as 0x0C4C7084 and use of fp->cur_anim_frame, because these details are likely match-sensitive.
Signedness of dat_attrs pointers can matter even when the underlying field names look like iteration counts; this PR changed u32* to s32* and used casts.

A near-match in an Anim routine may be resolved by identifying the specific state-advance helper that the original code called rather than leaving a placeholder-like generic ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining call.
Target-item cleanup routines may need to reset both named target fields and adjacent/less-understood fighter fields such as x1A64 after notifying the item subsystem.
naming_conventions: Matched functions retain address-suffixed names such as ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC, ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868, and ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0 when semantic names are not yet known.
Kirby motion IDs use ftKb_MS_* names, with copied-character prefixes visible here: Ms for Marth-like copied SpecialN and Fe for Fire Emblem fallback.
The added local helpers use descriptive camelCase names getAirSpecialMotionId and getGroundSpecialMotionId rather than address-style names.
Fighter access follows the project convention Fighter* fp = GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use inline helper functions to shape codegen for repeated conditional motion-ID selection.
Match boolean construction explicitly: initialize has_destroy_type to false, set true only for the enumerated capture motion IDs, then pass it to the item callback.
Maintain exact order of side effects in cleanup: it_802F2E7C call before clearing fp->x1A64 and fp->target_item_gobj.
Try signed pointer types and explicit casts for dat_attrs fields when arithmetic comparisons in loops do not match.
For Fighter_ChangeMotionState matches, preserve exact literal arguments and current-frame forwarding: motion id, 0x0C4C7084, fp->cur_anim_frame, 1.0f, 0.0f, NULL.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported 4 new matches: ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B868 (+120 bytes), ftKb_SpecialNMs_8010B8E0 (+120 bytes), ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC (+116 bytes), and ftKb_MsSpecialNLoop_Anim (+3 bytes).
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename or further document ftKb_SpecialNYs_8010A8BC or fp->x1A64.
Search for other Kirby copied SpecialN routines using FTKIND_MARS to choose Ms versus Fe motion IDs.
Compare other ftKb loop anim routines where charge_iterations signedness affects matching.
Look for item cleanup patterns using it_802F2E7C and target_item_gobj in other fighter files.
